summary: Georgia declares its first drought response level as wildfires burn across the state. Record-breaking early voting turnout hit 35,000 on day one of primary elections. And the Hawks head to New York for a pivotal Game 5 against the Knicks with the series tied 2-2.

topStory.headline: Georgia Declares First Drought Response Level as Wildfires Burn Across 38,000 Acres
topStory.summary: State environmental officials have enacted Level 1 drought measures, requiring public water systems to launch conservation campaigns as southeast Georgia battles its largest wildfires in years.
topStory.body:
Georgia's Environmental Protection Division declared a statewide Drought Response Level 1 on April 27, signaling growing concern over dry conditions that have fueled massive wildfires in the southern part of the state.

The declaration requires public water systems to conduct information campaigns helping residents understand the need for conservation. While Level 1 is the lowest tier of drought response, it comes as firefighters battle blazes that have burned over 38,000 acres across Georgia this spring.

The Highway 82 fire in Brantley County and the Pineland Road fire in Clinch County remain the largest active wildfires. Governor Brian Kemp visited south Georgia Tuesday to assess damage and meet with crews on the front lines. Weekend rain brought some relief, slowing containment efforts, but dry conditions and high temperatures continue to strain resources.

In metro Atlanta, the drought has impacted gardeners and increased fire risk in Fulton County. Fire crews are asking residents to avoid outdoor burning and practice water conservation as the state monitors conditions.
topStory.imageAlt: Firefighters battling brush fire in south Georgia

stories.headline: CNN Center's World Cup Transformation Into Apartments Takes Shape Downtown
stories.summary: The former CNN headquarters is being converted to residential use ahead of FIFA World Cup matches at Mercedes-Benz Stadium, part of Downtown's pre-tournament facelift.
stories.body:
The former CNN Center is undergoing a major transformation into apartments as Atlanta prepares to host eight FIFA World Cup matches this summer. The conversion is part of a broader effort to reimagine Downtown's built environment before the global spotlight arrives.

The development joins a wave of residential projects targeting the area around Mercedes-Benz Stadium and Centennial Olympic Park. With the World Cup kicking off June 15, developers are racing to complete renovations that will add housing stock to a district that has historically struggled to attract residents.

The project reflects a larger trend in Downtown Atlanta, where older office and commercial buildings are being converted to residential use. The transformation of such an iconic building signals confidence in Downtown's long-term residential prospects.
stories.imageAlt: CNN Center building in Downtown Atlanta

stories.headline: Ponce City Market Unveils Redesigned Rooftop Golf Course for Spring
stories.summary: The BeltLine destination has enlisted a big-name designer to revamp its rooftop mini-golf experience, adding to the entertainment options at one of Atlanta's most popular gathering spots.
stories.body:
Ponce City Market has revealed a redesigned mini-golf course on The Roof, its rooftop entertainment destination overlooking the Atlanta skyline and BeltLine Eastside Trail.

The renovation brings fresh design elements to one of the city's most visited rooftop attractions. The new course joins the existing lineup of carnival-style games, food vendors, and the observation deck that draws visitors year-round.

The Roof at Ponce City Market has become a signature Atlanta experience since opening, offering views of Midtown's skyline and direct access to the BeltLine. The investment in the mini-golf course suggests continued confidence in the development's ability to draw foot traffic in an increasingly competitive entertainment landscape.
stories.imageAlt: Rooftop golf course at Ponce City Market

stories.headline: UrbA ATL Project Restarts After Year of Idleness Near Atlantic Station
stories.summary: The 17-story mixed-use development has "remobilized" after sitting dormant for more than a year, adding to West Midtown's growing skyline.
stories.body:
Construction has restarted at UrbA ATL, a 17-story mixed-use development near Atlantic Station that had sat idle for more than a year. The project's remobilization is a positive sign for a part of West Midtown that has seen significant investment in recent years.

The development is part of the broader transformation of the industrial corridors west of Midtown proper, where warehouses and surface parking lots have given way to apartments, restaurants, and retail. The Howell Mill and 17th Street areas have become some of Atlanta's most sought-after addresses.

The pause in construction had raised questions about the project's future, but the return to activity suggests financing or other issues have been resolved. The development will add residential units to an area where demand has consistently outpaced supply.
stories.imageAlt: UrbA ATL construction site near Atlantic Station

stories.headline: Atlanta Restaurant Closures Reveal a Shifting Dining Economy
stories.summary: From neighborhood soul food joints to buzzy Inman Park concepts, restaurant closures are reshaping the city's food scene as costs rise and consumer habits change.
stories.body:
Atlanta has always worn its food scene like a badge of honor. From neighborhood soul food joints to buzzy new concepts in Inman Park, dining out has long been central to the city's identity. But a wave of closures is revealing the pressures facing restaurant operators in 2026.

Rising labor costs, ingredient prices, and rent have squeezed margins across the industry. Meanwhile, consumer dining habits have shifted, with more meals eaten at home and delivery eating into dine-in traffic. The result is a culinary landscape in flux.

The closures span categories and neighborhoods, from casual spots to fine dining. For a city that has built much of its reputation on its restaurants, the trend raises questions about what the next chapter of Atlanta's food scene will look like.

stories.headline: Sweet Auburn Springfest Celebrates 40 Years of African American Culture and Community
stories.summary: The annual festival returns to Auburn Avenue this weekend, marking four decades of celebrating Black Atlanta through food, music, and community events.
stories.body:
Sweet Auburn Springfest is celebrating its 40th anniversary this year, bringing food, music, and community events to the historic Auburn Avenue corridor. The festival remains one of Atlanta's signature celebrations of African American culture and history.

The annual event draws thousands to the neighborhood that was once called "the richest Negro street in the world" by Forbes magazine. The festival features live entertainment, local vendors, and activities highlighting the area's rich heritage.

Sweet Auburn is home to the Martin Luther King Jr. National Historical Park, Ebenezer Baptist Church, and the Sweet Auburn Curb Market. The Springfest celebration reinforces the neighborhood's role as a cultural anchor for Black Atlanta, even as the area sees new investment and development.

stories.headline: Atlanta Film Festival Marks 50 Years With Opening Night Sell-Out at the Plaza Theatre
stories.summary: The milestone anniversary celebration kicked off with "Idiots" at Poncey-Highland's historic movie house, drawing raucous laughter from a packed crowd.
stories.body:
The 50th annual Atlanta Film Festival opened with a sold-out screening of "Idiots" at the Plaza Theatre on April 23, launching a milestone celebration for one of the Southeast's premier film events.

The Plaza Theatre, Atlanta's last independent movie house, provided a fitting venue for the anniversary kickoff. The historic Poncey-Highland theater has hosted countless festival premieres and remains a beloved anchor of the city's arts scene.

The Atlanta Film Festival has grown from a small local event into a major showcase for independent film, with screenings, workshops, and industry events spanning multiple venues across the city. The 50th anniversary programming includes retrospectives, special guests, and films from around the world.
stories.imageAlt: Plaza Theatre marquee showing Atlanta Film Festival

stories.headline: First Day of Early Voting Shatters Records With 35,000 Georgia Ballots Cast
stories.summary: Monday's primary election early voting saw record-breaking turnout as Georgians weigh in on competitive races for governor, U.S. Senate, and state offices.
stories.body:
More than 35,000 Georgians cast ballots on Monday, April 27, during the first day of early voting for the May 19 primary election, setting a new record for midterm primary turnout.

Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger announced the numbers, which reflect heightened interest in competitive races at nearly every level of government. Races for governor, U.S. Senate, and Congressional seats have drawn crowded fields in both parties.

The Atlanta Press Club and Georgia Public Broadcasting hosted debates Monday featuring both Democratic and Republican gubernatorial candidates as part of the Loudermilk-Young Debate Series. The debates highlighted sharp contrasts over taxes, the economy, and the role of outside money in Georgia politics.

Early voting continues through May 16 at locations across metro Atlanta and statewide.

stories.headline: Arson Suspect Accused of Setting 15 Fires Near Clarkston That Threatened Homes and Mosque
stories.summary: A man is in custody after allegedly starting brush fires Sunday night and Monday morning in a half-mile radius near Georgia State's Perimeter College campus.
stories.body:
DeKalb County officials announced an arrest Tuesday in connection with 15 brush fires set over a few hours Sunday night and Monday morning in the Clarkston area. The fires threatened homes, apartments, and a nearby mosque.

County CEO Lorraine Cochran-Johnson and fire officials said the fires were set within a half-mile radius near the campuses of Georgia State's Perimeter College and Georgia Piedmont Technical College. Fire crews responded to multiple calls overnight as the blazes spread through dry brush.

Police have not released a motive for the alleged arson spree. The incident comes as Georgia grapples with drought conditions that have increased fire risk across the state. The suspected arsonist faces multiple charges.

stories.headline: Oprah Winfrey Returns to Morehouse for Surprise Visit With Scholars Program Students
stories.summary: The media mogul and philanthropist surprised graduating seniors at the all-men's college during a pre-graduation celebration for scholarship recipients.
stories.body:
Media mogul and philanthropist Oprah Winfrey surprised Morehouse College students with a pop-up visit they won't soon forget. Over the weekend, seniors at the all-men's college gathered for a pre-graduation celebration when Winfrey made an unannounced appearance.

Winfrey has a long history with Morehouse through her scholarship programs supporting students at the historically Black college. Her surprise visit came during an emotional gathering for students preparing to graduate.

The moment captured the connection between Winfrey and the institution, reinforcing her commitment to education and the next generation of Black male leaders. Morehouse remains one of the nation's premier HBCUs, with a storied history of producing leaders in business, politics, and the arts.
stories.imageAlt: Oprah Winfrey at Morehouse College event

stories.headline: Hawks Head to Madison Square Garden for Pivotal Game 5 With Series Tied 2-2
stories.summary: Atlanta's playoff series against New York shifts back to Manhattan for a decisive game after the teams split their home schedules.
stories.body:
The Atlanta Hawks are back at Madison Square Garden on Tuesday for Game 5 of the first-round playoffs against the New York Knicks. After splitting their home schedules, the series is tied 2-2 heading into what could be a pivotal moment for both teams.

The Hawks won both of their home games at State Farm Arena, including Thursday's contest that featured a halftime performance by Soulja Boy and appearances by Pastor Troy. The home energy was palpable, but now Atlanta must win on the road to take control of the best-of-seven series.

A win Tuesday would give the Hawks a 3-2 lead heading back to Atlanta for Game 6. A loss would put them in must-win territory. For a young Hawks team that has exceeded expectations this postseason, the stakes couldn't be higher.
